title: Region annotation syntax: [T]<'a, 'b> mixes two generic parameter systems
description: ferrum-lang-ownership.md §1.2 shows: 'fn choose[T]<'a, 'b>(x: &'a T, y: &'b T, use_x: bool): &'a T'. This mixes two syntactic systems: [T] is Ferrum's generic parameter syntax, but <'a, 'b> is Rust's lifetime parameter syntax. Ferrum uses [T] for type parameters. If lifetime/region parameters have their own separate <...> syntax, the spec must explain why and how to parse a signature with both. More likely this is a spec error and the correct form is fn choose['a, 'b, T](...) with all generic params unified in [...]. A caller encountering this signature will not know how to read it.
